transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good morning, you're through to First National Bank. This is Raj speaking. Can I start by taking your name and date of birth for security?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Sure thing — Chloe Fitzpatrick, twenty-eighth of July 1996.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Chloe, identity confirmed. What's brought you in today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: My boyfriend sent me some money — two-fifty — through his Halifax account. He did it about two hours ago and I haven't seen it come in yet.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: For Faster Payments transfers between different banks, you'd normally see the money within two hours. Let me check your account for incoming transfers.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Please do, I want to make sure he sent it to the right sort code.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can see an incoming transfer of two hundred and fifty pounds — it's in final processing and should appear on your balance within the next ten to fifteen minutes.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh brilliant! So it did come through.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, it's there and on its way. If for any reason it doesn't show in the next twenty minutes, give us a call back with the payment reference and we can trace it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Not a problem. Anything else today?
